name: City Express Corp
districts_or_customers: Possibly NYC/Queens area; no confirmed NYC DOE OPT contract found
recent_news: No M&A or news identified; listed in Yellow Pages directory and possibly referenced on NYSDOT bus operator registry. Very small operation (~3 employees, ~$213K revenue).
fit_notes: Sub-scale Queens-based operator with only ~3 employees and ~$213K revenue; unclear if real school-bus contractor or van/special-needs sub-hauler. Too small to be a meaningful Summit tuck-in absent strategic NYC DOE route value.

name: M & E School Transportation
districts_or_customers: Likely Lee County Schools area (Lehigh Acres, FL); not confirmed as official Lee County contractor
recent_news: No M&A or news identified. Founded ~2018; very small single-tier operator (~9 employees, ~$378K revenue). Operates in a market with multiple small school-transport firms (A&S Transportation - National Express, Lisdie Montano, Boys And Girls Transport).
fit_notes: Sub-scale single-truck/van-class school transport operator in SW Florida. Founder-owned with potential succession angle, but too small to move the needle as a standalone tuck-in; could be a route-bolt to an existing Lee County footprint.

name: Tass Trans LLC
districts_or_customers: Possibly School District of Philadelphia special-needs/IEP contracts (unconfirmed)
recent_news: No public M&A or news. Registered at residential-style address (6453 Eastwood St, Philadelphia 19149). No website or public principals identified.
fit_notes: Likely a small SPED/IEP van contractor in Philadelphia; residential registration and lack of web presence suggest owner-operator scale. Unlikely to be material as a standalone tuck-in; could be relevant only as a route/contract acquisition in a Philly cluster build.

name: Wellness Transportation Svc
districts_or_customers: Likely NEMT/Medicaid transport, not school district contracts; small South Georgia (Grady County) operation
recent_news: No M&A or news identified. Name overlaps with multiple unrelated GA NEMT firms (Wellness Transport of Georgia in Johns Creek; Wellness Medical Transportation LLC) - subject company in Cairo, GA appears to be a separate, smaller NEMT operator.
fit_notes: NOT a fit. Appears to be non-emergency medical transport (NEMT), not a school-bus / pupil-transportation contractor. Out of scope for Summit's K-12 student-transport tuck-in thesis.

name: Entrusted Transport LLC
districts_or_customers: Tulsa OK, Oklahoma City OK, Fayetteville AR, Springfield MO metros; Medicaid/NEMT clients - not K-12 districts
recent_news: NEMTAC accredited; founder Jason George featured on 'The Hidden Workforce' podcast. Previously held roles at FC Tulsa and Tulsa Drillers. No M&A activity identified.
fit_notes: NOT a fit. Confirmed non-emergency medical transportation (NEMT) provider focused on Medicaid/healthcare access, not student transportation. 'Special needs' here refers to adult disability/mobility patients, not K-12 SPED. Out of scope for Summit.
